meta.note: Ten new current-events cards added. Comedy craft + real rooms. Combat still silent; civilian angles preferred.
comedyRules: never explain the joke; no TED closer
smirk test: if it only sounds smart, rewrite

signals.trend: hundreds line a field in England to watch a plane park
signals.whatItIs: Trump stopped at RAF Mildenhall on the way home from Turkey so service members could see the new Air Force One. Official posts show the plane, the base, and the excitement. The human story underneath is older than the aircraft: people gather to stand near a symbol of power.
signals.whyTrending: White House photos + UK stopover posts are everywhere tonight. Curiosity heat, not just partisan dunking — pilgrimage energy.
signals.take: people drove to a field to watch a plane park. we replaced the cathedral with an aircraft and kept the queue. progress.
signals.replyTake: same pilgrimage energy. different fuel type.

hunt.query: ("Air Force One" OR Mildenhall) (crowd OR troops OR landed OR base) min_faves:20 -filter:replies lang:en
hunt.why: altitude room — reply about the crowd/ritual, not the party line.

rooms.text: President Donald J. Trump lands at RAF Mildenhall in England aboard the former Air Force One before departing back to the United States.
rooms.why: official post, huge room — only land if the line is about pilgrimage/power, not campaign
rooms.replyTake: they brought the plane out so the base could see it up close. humans will line up for anything that looks expensive and doesn't talk back.

rooms.text: President Trump Lands at RAF Mildenhall with the New Air Force One! … Showed it off to our outstanding service members
rooms.why: mid room amplifying the base visit — still replyable with altitude
rooms.replyTake: service members got to stand next to the big plane. that's the whole post and somehow it still works every century.

signals.trend: US men win World Cup money, split it with the women by contract
signals.whatItIs: The US men's team earned roughly $16M in World Cup prize money. Under the equal-pay collective bargaining deal, a large share is split with the women's side (federation keeps a cut; the rest is shared). The story is less "who scored" and more "how the institution moves money between rosters."
signals.whyTrending: Sports business + fairness fight in one package. Live replies from people who care about both soccer and the paycheck.
signals.take: the men won $16M. the women get half by contract. fairness finally stopped being a speech and became payroll.
signals.replyTake: they put equality in the CBA so nobody had to feel it in real time.
signals.openXQuery: USMNT prize money equal pay women

hunt.query: ("prize money" OR "equal pay" OR USWNT OR USMNT) (World Cup OR split OR share) min_faves:20 -filter:replies lang:en
hunt.why: prefer sports-biz or mid accounts over pure culture-war bait.

rooms.text: The U.S. men’s $16 million World Cup prize money will be split equally with the women’s national team under an equal-pay deal.
rooms.why: active reply room on the equal-pay structure
rooms.replyTake: the check got written before the feelings meeting. rare W for paperwork.

rooms.text: The USMNT’s World Cup run earned $16M… under equal-pay, next year’s USWNT will receive $6.4M of it. Federation keeps 20%, rest split evenly.
rooms.why: business breakdown — room wants numbers + one sharp frame
rooms.replyTake: federation skims 20%, rest split by treaty. even the house cut looks honest for once.

signals.trend: fill up the tank — the ceasefire broke and oil noticed
signals.whatItIs: US–Iran fighting heated up again after a fragile ceasefire; markets and posters are talking gas prices, grocery knock-ons, and "fill up before it spikes." The civilian experience of geopolitics is still the pump and the receipt.
signals.whyTrending: War news is SILENT for dunk-combat. The gas-line angle is how ordinary people meet the story — and it's replyable without LARPing as a general.
signals.take: the ceasefire ended and gas remembered how to go up. foreign policy for people who don't read foreign policy.
signals.replyTake: geopolitics, but make it the pump.

hunt.query: ("gas" OR oil OR pump OR "fill up") (Iran OR prices OR skyrocket) min_faves:20 -filter:replies lang:en
hunt.why: civilian cost angle only — skip pure combat footage dunks.

rooms.text: America has launched a NEW WAVE of attacks on Iran. Don't forget to fill up you gas tanks before prices skyrocket!
rooms.why: mid room stating the civilian consequence — open for a dry line
rooms.replyTake: "fill up before prices skyrocket" is the most honest national security brief most of us will get.

rooms.text: Oil markets are rattling, prices climbing. You'll pay more at the pump and for groceries… Chaos isn't a strategy—it's just expensive.
rooms.why: smaller room, cost frame already set — don't re-litigate the war, land the cost line
rooms.replyTake: chaos isn't abstract. chaos is $4.89 and a receipt that feels personal.

signals.trend: Mexico–England pulled 21 million US viewers
signals.whatItIs: FOX says Mexico vs England became the most-watched non-USMNT men's World Cup telecast in English-language US history — about 21.7 million viewers. The tournament is not just on; it's a mass event again.
signals.whyTrending: Ratings flex posts. Culture story: soccer as American living-room infrastructure.
signals.take: 21.7 million Americans watched Mexico–England in English. "soccer isn't big here" can take the rest of the week off.
signals.replyTake: when the rating looks like a debate night, it's not a hobby anymore. it's weather.

hunt.query: (Mexico OR England) (viewers OR ratings OR "most-watched") World Cup min_faves:15 -filter:replies lang:en
hunt.why: culture/ratings room — one observation about mass attention.

rooms.text: Mexico vs. England… NEW most-watched non-USMNT FIFA Men’s World Cup telecast in English-language U.S. history with 21,742,000 viewers.
rooms.why: official ratings post — quieter than fan wars, good for a clean line
rooms.replyTake: 21.7 million. that's not growth. that's a country admitting it was already watching.

signals.trend: if anyone wins, Kane somehow deserves the Ballon d'Or
signals.whatItIs: A joke format is spreading: no matter which country wins the World Cup, Harry Kane deserves the Ballon d'Or. Pure football meme theology.
signals.whyTrending: Knockout round humor. Light, shareable, broad room.
signals.take: if Norway wins: Haaland. if anyone else wins: Kane. analysis has left the chat. theology has entered.
signals.replyTake: Kane is plot armor with a national team.

hunt.query: Kane ("Ballon" OR Ballon) (World Cup OR deserves) min_faves:30 -filter:replies lang:en
hunt.why: meme room — land the joke, don't explain it.

rooms.text: If Norway wins… Haaland. If France/England/Morocco/Belgium/Spain/Argentina/Switzerland wins… Kane. Simple.
rooms.why: hot meme thread, mid-size author, replies already forming
rooms.replyTake: this isn't a ranking system. it's a loyalty program.

signals.trend: fake email jobs that pay poverty wages
signals.whatItIs: Counter-meme to the "easy email job that pays well" dream: lots of email jobs are empty *and* poorly paid — alienation without the compensation.
signals.whyTrending: Meaning-crisis adjacent, always simmering, works as universal spine.
signals.take: people romanticize fake email jobs that pay. nobody romanticizes fake email jobs that also pay nothing. that's just employment.
signals.replyTake: alienation with benefits was the premium tier. you found the free trial.

hunt.query: ("fake email job" OR "email job") min_faves:15 -filter:replies lang:en

rooms.text: People always talk about fake email jobs that pay well but no one talks about fake email jobs that pay poverty wages
rooms.why: clean thesis, small open room
rooms.replyTake: the fake email job discourse forgot a whole pricing tier: empty and broke.

rooms.text: Burnout was not nearly as common in prior generations… less attainable while expectations get higher… Or was it because of the cocaine
rooms.why: meaning + humor already loaded
rooms.replyTake: burnout either rose with expectations or fell with the cocaine. both sound correct and I hate that.

signals.trend: adult knowledge expires faster than milk
signals.whatItIs: A thread on how professional knowledge depreciates: being elite at "content marketing 2015" means almost nothing now except fundamentals like make things people want.
signals.whyTrending: Quiet meaning/work room — not headline news, but live conversation for the right replies.
signals.take: being elite at content marketing in 2015 is like being elite at MySpace themes. the trophy still exists. the sport doesn't.
signals.replyTake: skills now come with an expiration date and no fridge.

hunt.query: (knowledge OR skills OR career) (depreciat OR obsolete OR "used to know") min_faves:15 -filter:replies lang:en
hunt.why: meaning room — one hard line, no hustle-coach gloss.

rooms.text: dawg I knew so much about content marketing in 2015… all of the alpha is completely gone apart from fundamentals like "make things people want"
rooms.why: scene-adjacent mid-large, good reply room
rooms.replyTake: top 0.01% of 2015 content marketing is a museum exhibit with a gift shop that only sells "make things people want."

signals.trend: Haaland has more Instagram followers than his club
signals.whatItIs: During World Cup heat, Haaland reportedly passed Manchester City in Instagram followers — personal brand over institution in public.
signals.whyTrending: Sports + platform economy. Strong universal spine post.
signals.take: Haaland has more Instagram followers than Manchester City. the employee beat the company at the only job the company still understands.
signals.replyTake: the crest is still on the shirt. the audience is not.

hunt.query: Haaland (Instagram OR followers) (City OR club) min_faves:30 -filter:replies lang:en
hunt.why: optional reply; better as your own post.

rooms.text: Erling Haaland now has more Instagram followers than Manchester City… popularity surges amid the World Cup
rooms.why: if replying: institutional angle only
rooms.replyTake: personal brand just outranked the institution on the scoreboard that actually pays. feudalism, but the peasant has a better camera.

signals.trend: your network beats your follower count
signals.whatItIs: Creator-economy wallpaper: the right humans beat vanity metrics. Still true, still under-monetizable, still restated daily.
signals.whyTrending: Evergreen. Good original post if you need a non-sports spine.
signals.take: your network is more valuable than your follower count. the least profitable true sentence on this app, which is why it never becomes a course.
signals.replyTake: true since villages. still won't sell a Notion template.

rooms.text: Hot take: your network is more valuable than your follower count
rooms.why: only if your line is sharper than "so true"
rooms.replyTake: hot take from 10,000 BC still clearing. the follower count is just a census with worse friends.

signals.trend: Grok 4.5 launched (one card — optional)
signals.whatItIs: xAI released Grok 4.5 today. Builders are loud about it. Kept as a single optional card so the feed is not AI-first.
signals.whyTrending: Tech launch day. Skip unless you specifically want that room.
signals.take: the guy who owns the website said his AI is the best AI and the website agreed. journalism is healing.
signals.replyTake: finally a model faster than my attention span. progress.

rooms.text: first impression of Grok 4.5: harness very good and responsive; extremely fast
rooms.why: only if you want one tech reply today
rooms.replyTake: the harness is so fast I don't have time to open a second tab and forget why I opened the first one. disruptive.

signals.trend: US–Iran ceasefire collapse and new strikes
signals.whatItIs: Reporting: fragile US–Iran ceasefire broke down; US strikes on Iranian targets after attacks on shipping; Iran responses; NATO-summit context; oil markets moving. Active conflict.
signals.whyTrending: Hard news. Do not use for brand growth dunks. Civilian cost lives in the gas card above; combat stays silent.

signals.trend: birthright citizenship fight returns to the feed
signals.whatItIs: Partisan posts demanding Supreme Court rehearing on birthright citizenship after billboards in Mexico about birthing centers. High heat, low good-faith room.
signals.whyTrending: Culture-war accelerant. Skip for growth.

signals.trend: Midtown evacuations and other emergency local news
signals.whatItIs: NYC construction safety scare and other emergency/local stories still circulating. Not for commentary growth.
signals.whyTrending: Local emergency. Silent.

signals.trend: a day with no World Cup game and the feed feels unfinished
signals.whatItIs: Knockout rounds create off-days. People are posting that the day feels empty without a match — the tournament has become the default calendar.
signals.whyTrending: Rest-day malaise posts. Broad, light, easy room.
signals.take: no World Cup today and people are reporting emptiness like someone canceled Christmas. the sport became infrastructure.
signals.replyTake: the calendar had a hole in it and everyone felt the draft.

hunt.query: ("no World Cup" OR "without a World Cup" OR "no game") (empty OR today) min_faves:20 -filter:replies lang:en
hunt.why: rest-day posts — land one dry line, don't overdo soccer fan voice.

rooms.text: today felt so empty without a World Cup game.
rooms.why: small open room, pure feeling post
rooms.replyTake: the match schedule is doing more for national mood than most legislation. rest day hit different.

rooms.text: Empty stadiums. No World Cup. What am I supposed to do today?
rooms.why: active replies already — easy to join
rooms.replyTake: touch grass is the official FIFA rest-day protocol and nobody wants it.

signals.trend: Mexico fans briefly become Norway fans for spite
signals.whatItIs: After England knocked Mexico out, some Mexico fans are publicly rooting for Norway (and Haaland) to eliminate England next. Classic enemy-of-my-enemy fandom.
signals.whyTrending: Elimination spite is peak tournament culture.
signals.take: Mexico just dual-citizened into Norway for one match. geopolitics, but make it spite.
signals.replyTake: the enemy of my enemy gets a temporary jersey and zero loyalty after full time.

hunt.query: (Mexico OR Mexican) (Norway OR Haaland) (England OR spite OR rooting) min_faves:20 -filter:replies lang:en
hunt.why: fan culture room — one clean joke.

rooms.text: Some Mexico fans are now rooting for Norway to eliminate England… England eliminated Mexico… want Haaland and Norway to knock England out next.
rooms.why: clean summary post of the meme
rooms.replyTake: international relations but the only treaty is spite and it expires at the final whistle.

signals.trend: Boston turns red and green for Morocco
signals.whatItIs: Moroccan fans flooded Boston streets ahead of the World Cup quarter-final against France. Diaspora city becomes temporary capital of a match.
signals.whyTrending: Street energy + tournament stakes. Visual, communal.
signals.take: a whole city block just became an away end. borders are legal. fandom is logistics.
signals.replyTake: the quarter-final started in the street a few hours early. kickoff is a formality.

hunt.query: (Morocco OR Moroccan) (Boston OR streets OR fans) (World Cup OR France) min_faves:20 -filter:replies lang:en
hunt.why: atmosphere posts — celebrate the crowd, not the score prediction.

rooms.text: Boston is turning red and green. Thousands of Moroccan fans have already taken over the streets ahead of Morocco’s World Cup quarter-final against France.
rooms.why: scene-setting with replies forming
rooms.replyTake: nation-state on loan to a few blocks of Boston. return policy: after full time.

signals.trend: government-run gas stations vs the price at the pump
signals.whatItIs: White House hyped a 'Freedom Fuel' gas station in Philadelphia at $3.47. Critics called government-run gas stations dumber than government-run groceries. The fight is about who is allowed to touch the pump narrative.
signals.whyTrending: Culture-war economics you can feel in the car. Better than pure combat dunks.
signals.take: we got a branded government gas station and the replies are a philosophy seminar about who should be allowed near a nozzle.
signals.replyTake: the pump is now a press conference with unleaded.

hunt.query: ("Freedom Fuel" OR "government-run gas" OR "gas station") (Trump OR government OR grocery) min_faves:20 -filter:replies lang:en
hunt.why: policy comedy room — dry line, no party jersey required.

rooms.text: Government-run gas stations is somehow a stupider idea than government-run grocery stores.
rooms.why: mid-large, active replies, clean premise
rooms.replyTake: we ran out of normal political arguments and started ranking which state-owned aisle is dumber. that's how you know summer's going well.

signals.trend: $8.99 cherries and the grocery receipt as national mood
signals.whatItIs: People posting specific grocery shocks — cherries near $9 a bag — as proof the country is sideways. Cost of living as folk journalism.
signals.whyTrending: Always on. Specific prices travel farther than inflation charts.
signals.take: the state of the union is a bag of cherries for $8.99. no speech required.
signals.replyTake: macroeconomics, but make it produce aisle.

hunt.query: (grocery OR cherries OR rent) (prices OR "going to hell" OR expensive) min_faves:15 -filter:replies lang:en
hunt.why: personal cost posts — match the receipt energy.

rooms.text: just paid $8.99 for a bag of cherries at the grocery store y’all this country is going to hell in a hand basket
rooms.why: specific price = load-bearing detail
rooms.replyTake: $8.99 cherries is the most bipartisan foreign policy brief of the week.

rooms.text: Grocery prices are expected to increase again. Rents are up again…
rooms.why: cost stack in one post
rooms.replyTake: groceries and rent filed a joint motion to keep the plot simple.

signals.trend: how many tankers crossed Hormuz: 0, 00, or 000
signals.whatItIs: With Iran conflict and Hormuz tension, people are half-joking about oil tanker traffic going to zero. Trade route as punchline for war risk.
signals.whyTrending: Shipping + oil anxiety without requiring combat cosplay.
signals.take: the global economy is refreshing a shipping tracker like it's a group chat that went silent.
signals.replyTake: "0, 00, or 000" is the most accurate situation report of the night.

hunt.query: (Hormuz OR tanker OR tankers) (oil OR closed OR zero OR traffic) min_faves:20 -filter:replies lang:en

rooms.text: How many oil tankers crossed the Strait of Hormuz in the past 72 hours? Very curious to know whether 0, 00, or 000
rooms.why: dry question, good for a flat reply
rooms.replyTake: the three-digit precision really sells the confidence.

signals.trend: everyone's job title is Entrepreneur and the rent is still $120 each
signals.whatItIs: Parody of hustle culture: twenty friends rename themselves entrepreneurs while splitting a 3-bedroom. Status language vs material life.
signals.whyTrending: Creator-economy satire that always clears.
signals.take: we nationalized the word entrepreneur and the only thing that scaled was the roommate count.
signals.replyTake: title inflation hit harder than wage inflation.

hunt.query: (entrepreneur OR "job title") (rent OR roommates OR house) min_faves:20 -filter:replies lang:en

rooms.text: POV: You and your 20 closest friends all changed their job title to "Entrepreneur" and now pay $120 a month in rent to split a 3 bedroom house
rooms.why: parody account, joke already loaded — add one notch
rooms.replyTake: the LinkedIn headline scaled. the square footage did not.

signals.trend: The Odyssey is a myth and people are fighting about casting again
signals.whatItIs: Debate around reinterpretations of Homeric myth (Nolan Odyssey discourse, casting history). One side: myths get remade forever. Other side: culture-war literacy tests.
signals.whyTrending: Evergreen myth fight with a current hook. Fits meaning/culture lane.
signals.take: humans have been recasting The Odyssey since before film existed and still act shocked when someone does it again. the poem is the open-source project.
signals.replyTake: myths that can't be retold aren't myths. they're museum labels.

hunt.query: (Odyssey OR Homer OR myth) (casting OR retell OR illiterate OR reinterpret) min_faves:30 -filter:replies lang:en
hunt.why: culture room — one line about retelling, not pile-on.

rooms.text: The Odyssey is a long-form poem about a myth… long history of being reinterpreted… Today's critics are culturally illiterate buffoons.
rooms.why: huge room — only if the line is about myth-as-software, not dunking individuals
rooms.replyTake: if a story survived oral tradition it can survive a casting announcement. the panic is the only new part.

signals.trend: Mitch McConnell spotted at a Noah Kahan concert (he's fine)
signals.whatItIs: After hospitalization chatter, a joke post claims McConnell was at a Noah Kahan show at Fenway and is fine. Political grimness meets soft-rock logistics.
signals.whyTrending: Dark humor + celebrity-politician collision.
signals.take: the health update format is now a concert review. democracy is healing, but the setlist is sad.
signals.replyTake: "he's fine" delivered via Noah Kahan is the most 2026 medical bulletin possible.

hunt.query: McConnell (concert OR Kahan OR Fenway OR fine) min_faves:15 -filter:replies lang:en
hunt.why: joke room — stay light, no health ghoulishness.

rooms.text: I just went to the Noah Kahan concert at Fenway with Mitch McConnell. He’s fine.
rooms.why: bit already planted
rooms.replyTake: most normal bipartisan hang of the decade and I refuse to fact-check it.

signals.trend: Mexico's send-off video and the tournament hangover
signals.whatItIs: Emotional video of Mexico players thanking fans after their World Cup run. Elimination ritual: gratitude reel, then silence until the next cycle.
signals.whyTrending: Post-elimination content always spikes for big fanbases.
signals.take: the exit interview is a thank-you reel now. the nation cries on schedule and means it.
signals.replyTake: elimination comes with a credits sequence. sports learned prestige TV.
signals.openXQuery: Mexico World Cup thank fans video

hunt.query: Mexico (thank OR fans OR emotional) (World Cup OR players) min_faves:30 -filter:replies lang:en
hunt.why: emotional fan room — soft take or skip if too tender.

rooms.text: An emotional video featuring every Mexico player who participated at the 2026 World Cup thanking the fans…
rooms.why: big emotion post — reply only if the line is gentle-funny not cruel
rooms.replyTake: the tournament ends and the thank-you video ships like a patch note. human, efficient, devastating.
